headline: Childhood Cancer Survival Rate Lags Regional Peers at 52%
context: Malaysia's 5-year childhood cancer survival rate of 52% trails Singapore's 82% and Thailand's 65%.

cards.big: Only 52% of Malaysian children with cancer survive 5 years versus 82% in Singapore
cards.sub: Late diagnosis and treatment delays account for most of the survival gap

cards.big: Average time from first symptom to cancer diagnosis in children is 14 weeks in Malaysia
cards.sub: In high-income countries the same diagnostic journey takes 4-6 weeks on average

cards.big: Only 3 government hospitals have dedicated pediatric oncology units nationwide
cards.sub: Children in East Malaysia must travel to Peninsula for specialized cancer treatment

cards.big: WHO estimates 80% of childhood cancers are curable with timely access to standard treatment
cards.sub: Survival gap represents preventable death from systemic access failure not medical limitation

cards.big: Every child who dies from a curable cancer died from a system failure not the disease itself
Should every state have at least one dedicated pediatric oncology center
